## nvidia.com/v1alpha1


Package v1alpha1 contains API Schema definitions for the nvidia.com v1alpha1 API group.

This package defines the DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest (DGDR) custom resource, which provides
a high-level, SLA-driven interface for deploying machine learning models on Dynamo.

Package v1alpha1 contains API Schema definitions for the nvidia.com v1alpha1 API group.

### Resource Types
- [DynamoCheckpoint](#dynamocheckpoint)
- [DynamoComponentDeployment](#dynamocomponentdeployment)
- [DynamoGraphDeployment](#dynamographdeployment)
- [DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest](#dynamographdeploymentrequest)
- [DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter](#dynamographdeploymentscalingadapter)
- [DynamoModel](#dynamomodel)

### CheckpointDeletionPolicy

_Underlying type: `string`_

CheckpointDeletionPolicy defines what happens to DGD-managed automatic
checkpoint resources when the owning DGD is deleted.

**Appears in:** [ServiceCheckpointConfig](#servicecheckpointconfig)

<ParamField path="Delete">
  CheckpointDeletionPolicyDelete deletes DGD-managed automatic checkpoint<br />CRs and artifacts when the owning DGD is deleted.<br />
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="Retain">
  CheckpointDeletionPolicyRetain keeps DGD-managed automatic checkpoint CRs<br />and artifacts after the owning DGD is deleted. Users can reference the<br />retained checkpoint with checkpointRef if they accept compatibility risk.<br />
</ParamField>

### CheckpointMode

_Underlying type: `string`_

Deprecated: use checkpoint.enabled instead.
enabled=true without checkpointRef creates a DGD-managed automatic
checkpoint; checkpointRef restores the named checkpoint.

**Appears in:** [ServiceCheckpointConfig](#servicecheckpointconfig)

<ParamField path="Auto">
  Deprecated: use checkpoint.enabled=true and omit checkpointRef.<br />
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="Manual">
  Deprecated: use checkpointRef to restore an existing checkpoint.<br />
</ParamField>

### CheckpointStartupPolicy

_Underlying type: `string`_

CheckpointStartupPolicy defines when worker pods should wait for a checkpoint.

**Appears in:** [ServiceCheckpointConfig](#servicecheckpointconfig)

<ParamField path="Immediate">
  CheckpointStartupPolicyImmediate starts workers immediately. The checkpoint<br />job runs in the background, and only pods created after the checkpoint is<br />Ready are restore-shaped by the pod-create mutating webhook.<br />
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="WaitForCheckpoint">
  CheckpointStartupPolicyWaitForCheckpoint gates worker replicas until the<br />component's checkpoint is Ready, then starts them from the checkpoint.<br />
</ParamField>

### ComponentKind

_Underlying type: `string`_

ComponentKind represents the type of underlying Kubernetes resource.

**Appears in:** [ServiceReplicaStatus](#servicereplicastatus)

</ParamField>

### ConfigMapKeySelector

ConfigMapKeySelector selects a specific key from a ConfigMap.
Used to reference external configuration data stored in ConfigMaps.

**Appears in:** [ProfilingConfigSpec](#profilingconfigspec)

<ParamField path="name" type="string" required={true}>
  Name of the ConfigMap containing the desired data.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="key" type="string" default="disagg.yaml">
  Key in the ConfigMap to select. If not specified, defaults to "disagg.yaml".
</ParamField>

_Underlying type: `string`_

### DeploymentOverridesSpec

DeploymentOverridesSpec allows users to customize metadata for auto-created DynamoGraphDeployments.
When autoApply is enabled, these overrides are applied to the generated DGD resource.

**Appears in:** [DynamoGraphDeploymentRequestSpec](#dynamographdeploymentrequestspec)

<ParamField path="name" type="string">
  Name is the desired name for the created DynamoGraphDeployment.<br />If not specified, defaults to the DGDR name.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="namespace" type="string">
  Namespace is the desired namespace for the created DynamoGraphDeployment.<br />If not specified, defaults to the DGDR namespace.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="labels" type="map[string]string">
  Labels are additional labels to add to the DynamoGraphDeployment metadata.<br />These are merged with auto-generated labels from the profiling process.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="annotations" type="map[string]string">
  Annotations are additional annotations to add to the DynamoGraphDeployment metadata.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="workersImage" type="string">
  WorkersImage specifies the container image to use for DynamoGraphDeployment worker components.<br />This image is used for both temporary DGDs created during online profiling and the final DGD.<br />If omitted, the image from the base config file (e.g., disagg.yaml) is used.<br />Example: "nvcr.io/nvidia/ai-dynamo/vllm-runtime:1.2.1"
</ParamField>

### DeploymentStatus

DeploymentStatus tracks the state of an auto-created DynamoGraphDeployment.
This status is populated when autoApply is enabled and a DGD is created.

**Appears in:** [DynamoGraphDeploymentRequestStatus](#dynamographdeploymentrequeststatus)

<ParamField path="name" type="string">
  Name is the name of the created DynamoGraphDeployment.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="namespace" type="string">
  Namespace is the namespace of the created DynamoGraphDeployment.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="state" type="DGDState" default="initializing">
  State is the current state of the DynamoGraphDeployment.<br />This value is mirrored from the DGD's status.state field.

  <span className="enum-values"><span className="enum-label">Allowed values:</span> <Badge intent="note" minimal>initializing</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>pending</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>successful</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>failed</Badge></span>
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="created" type="boolean">
  Created indicates whether the DGD has been successfully created.<br />Used to prevent recreation if the DGD is manually deleted by users.
</ParamField>

### DynamoCheckpoint

DynamoCheckpoint is the Schema for the dynamocheckpoints API
It represents a container checkpoint that can be used to restore pods to a warm state

<ParamField path="apiVersion" type="string">
  `nvidia.com/v1alpha1`
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="kind" type="string">
  `DynamoCheckpoint`
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="metadata" type="ObjectMeta">
  Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of `metadata`.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="spec" type="DynamoCheckpointSpec" />
<ParamField path="status" type="DynamoCheckpointStatus" />

### DynamoCheckpointIdentity

Deprecated: legacy identity metadata. Keep it only where v1alpha1 still
requires spec.identity; omit DGD-managed identity and use checkpointRef for
explicit restores.

**Appears in:** [DynamoCheckpointSpec](#dynamocheckpointspec) · [ServiceCheckpointConfig](#servicecheckpointconfig)

<ParamField path="model" type="string" required={true}>
  Model is the model identifier (e.g., "meta-llama/Llama-3-70B")<br />Deprecated: legacy spec.identity only.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="backendFramework" type="string" required={true}>
  BackendFramework is the runtime framework (vllm, sglang, trtllm)<br />Deprecated: legacy spec.identity only.

  <span className="enum-values"><span className="enum-label">Allowed values:</span> <Badge intent="note" minimal>vllm</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>sglang</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>trtllm</Badge></span>
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="dynamoVersion" type="string">
  DynamoVersion is the Dynamo platform version (optional).<br />Deprecated: legacy spec.identity only.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="tensorParallelSize" type="integer" default="1">
  TensorParallelSize is the tensor parallel configuration.<br />Deprecated: checkpoint launch uses the pod template instead.

  _Minimum: 1_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="pipelineParallelSize" type="integer" default="1">
  PipelineParallelSize is the pipeline parallel configuration.<br />Deprecated: checkpoint launch uses the pod template instead.

  _Minimum: 1_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="dtype" type="string">
  Dtype is the data type (fp16, bf16, fp8, etc.).<br />Deprecated: legacy spec.identity only.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="maxModelLen" type="integer">
  MaxModelLen is the maximum sequence length.<br />Deprecated: legacy spec.identity only.

  _Minimum: 1_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="extraParameters" type="map[string]string">
  ExtraParameters are additional parameters that affect the checkpoint hash.<br />Use for any framework-specific or custom parameters not covered above.<br />Deprecated: legacy spec.identity only.
</ParamField>

### DynamoCheckpointJobConfig

DynamoCheckpointJobConfig defines the configuration for the checkpoint creation Job

**Appears in:** [DynamoCheckpointSpec](#dynamocheckpointspec)

<ParamField path="podTemplateSpec" type="PodTemplateSpec" required={true}>
  PodTemplateSpec allows customizing the checkpoint Job pod<br />This should include the container that runs the workload to be checkpointed<br />and any workload/runtime env, service account, GMS, or DRA wiring needed<br />by that container. Auto-created checkpoints from DynamoGraphDeployment<br />render Dynamo defaults before creating the DynamoCheckpoint.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="targetContainerName" type="string" default="main">
  TargetContainerName is the container in PodTemplateSpec to snapshot.

  _MaxLength: 63; MinLength: 1; Pattern: `^[a-z0-9]([-a-z0-9]*[a-z0-9])?$`_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="sharedMemory" type="SharedMemorySpec">
  SharedMemory controls the tmpfs mounted at /dev/shm for the checkpoint Job pod.<br />When omitted, checkpoint Jobs use the same default 8Gi tmpfs as Dynamo components.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="activeDeadlineSeconds" type="integer" default="3600">
  ActiveDeadlineSeconds specifies the maximum time the Job can run

  _Minimum: 1_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="backoffLimit" type="integer">
  Deprecated: BackoffLimit is ignored. Checkpoint Jobs never retry.

  _Minimum: 0_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="ttlSecondsAfterFinished" type="integer">
  Deprecated: TTLSecondsAfterFinished is ignored. Checkpoint Jobs use a fixed<br />300 second TTL.

  _Minimum: 0_
</ParamField>

### DynamoCheckpointPhase

_Underlying type: `string`_

DynamoCheckpointPhase represents the current phase of the checkpoint lifecycle

**Appears in:** [DynamoCheckpointStatus](#dynamocheckpointstatus)

<ParamField path="Pending">
  DynamoCheckpointPhasePending indicates the checkpoint CR has been created but the Job has not started<br />
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="Creating">
  DynamoCheckpointPhaseCreating indicates the checkpoint Job is running<br />
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="Ready">
  DynamoCheckpointPhaseReady indicates the checkpoint artifact is available<br />
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="Failed">
  DynamoCheckpointPhaseFailed indicates the checkpoint creation failed<br />
</ParamField>

### DynamoCheckpointSpec

DynamoCheckpointSpec defines the desired state of DynamoCheckpoint

**Appears in:** [DynamoCheckpoint](#dynamocheckpoint)

<ParamField path="identity" type="DynamoCheckpointIdentity" required={true}>
  Deprecated: required by v1alpha1 for standalone checkpoints. Auto<br />checkpoints synthesize it; checkpointRef restores use the referenced CR.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="gpuMemoryService" type="GPUMemoryServiceSpec">
  GPUMemoryService records checkpoint-time GPU Memory Service metadata for<br />a prepared checkpoint Job pod. The DynamoCheckpoint controller does not<br />inject GMS/DRA resources; auto-created checkpoints from<br />DynamoGraphDeployment prepare the pod template before creating this object.<br />Manual GMS-enabled checkpoints must provide the prepared pod template; the<br />controller fails the checkpoint if the required GMS/DRA wiring is missing.<br />This field is intentionally outside spec.identity, so it does not affect<br />the checkpoint identity hash or deduplication.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="job" type="DynamoCheckpointJobConfig" required={true}>
  Job defines the configuration for the checkpoint creation Job
</ParamField>

### DynamoCheckpointStatus

DynamoCheckpointStatus defines the observed state of DynamoCheckpoint

**Appears in:** [DynamoCheckpoint](#dynamocheckpoint)

<ParamField path="phase" type="DynamoCheckpointPhase">
  Phase represents the current phase of the checkpoint lifecycle

  <span className="enum-values"><span className="enum-label">Allowed values:</span> <Badge intent="note" minimal>Pending</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>Creating</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>Ready</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>Failed</Badge></span>
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="checkpointID" type="string">
  CheckpointID is the artifact ID used by the snapshot protocol.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="identityHash" type="string">
  IdentityHash is the computed hash of the checkpoint identity.<br />Deprecated: use CheckpointID. This field is retained for compatibility<br />with older status consumers.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="location" type="string">
  Deprecated: Location is ignored and no longer populated. It is retained<br />only so older objects continue to validate.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="storageType" type="DynamoCheckpointStorageType">
  Deprecated: StorageType is ignored and no longer populated. It is retained<br />only so older objects continue to validate.

  <span className="enum-values"><span className="enum-label">Allowed values:</span> <Badge intent="note" minimal>pvc</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>s3</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>oci</Badge></span>
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="jobName" type="string">
  JobName is the name of the checkpoint creation Job
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="createdAt" type="Time">
  CreatedAt is the timestamp when the checkpoint became ready
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="message" type="string">
  Message provides additional information about the current state
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="conditions" type="[]Condition">
  DEPRECATED: Conditions are deprecated. Use status.phase instead.
</ParamField>

### DynamoCheckpointStorageType

_Underlying type: `string`_

Deprecated: StorageType is retained for compatibility with older
DynamoCheckpoint status consumers. The current checkpoint flow publishes
PVC-backed artifacts discovered from the snapshot-agent DaemonSet.

**Appears in:** [DynamoCheckpointStatus](#dynamocheckpointstatus)

DynamoComponentDeployment is the Schema for the dynamocomponentdeployments API

<ParamField path="apiVersion" type="string">
  `nvidia.com/v1alpha1`
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="kind" type="string">
  `DynamoComponentDeployment`

  Spec defines the desired state for this Dynamo component deployment.

### DynamoComponentDeploymentSharedSpec

**Appears in:** [DynamoComponentDeploymentSpec](#dynamocomponentdeploymentspec) · [DynamoGraphDeploymentSpec](#dynamographdeploymentspec)

<ParamField path="annotations" type="map[string]string">
  Annotations to add to generated Kubernetes resources for this component<br />(such as Pod, Service, and Ingress when applicable).
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="labels" type="map[string]string">
  Labels to add to generated Kubernetes resources for this component.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="serviceName" type="string">
  The name of the component
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="componentType" type="string">
  ComponentType indicates the role of this component (for example, "main").
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="subComponentType" type="string">
  SubComponentType indicates the sub-role of this component (for example, "prefill").
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="globalDynamoNamespace" type="boolean">
  GlobalDynamoNamespace indicates that the Component will be placed in the global Dynamo namespace
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="resources" type="Resources">
  Resources requested and limits for this component, including CPU, memory,<br />GPUs/devices, and any runtime-specific resources.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="autoscaling" type="Autoscaling">
  Deprecated: This field is deprecated and ignored. Use DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter<br />with HPA, KEDA, or Planner for autoscaling instead. See docs/kubernetes/autoscaling.md<br />for migration guidance. This field will be removed in a future API version.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="envs" type="[]EnvVar">
  Envs defines additional environment variables to inject into the component containers.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="envFromSecret" type="string">
  EnvFromSecret references a Secret whose key/value pairs will be exposed as<br />environment variables in the component containers.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="volumeMounts" type="[]VolumeMount">
  VolumeMounts references PVCs defined at the top level for volumes to be mounted by the component.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="ingress" type="IngressSpec">
  Ingress config to expose the component outside the cluster (or through a service mesh).
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="modelRef" type="ModelReference">
  ModelRef references a model that this component serves<br />When specified, a headless service will be created for endpoint discovery
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="sharedMemory" type="SharedMemorySpec">
  SharedMemory controls the tmpfs mounted at /dev/shm (enable/disable and size).
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="extraPodMetadata" type="ExtraPodMetadata">
  ExtraPodMetadata adds labels/annotations to the created Pods.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="extraPodSpec" type="ExtraPodSpec">
  ExtraPodSpec allows to override the main pod spec configuration.<br />It is a k8s standard PodSpec. It also contains a MainContainer (standard k8s Container) field<br />that allows overriding the main container configuration.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="livenessProbe" type="Probe">
  LivenessProbe to detect and restart unhealthy containers.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="readinessProbe" type="Probe">
  ReadinessProbe to signal when the container is ready to receive traffic.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="replicas" type="integer">
  Replicas is the desired number of Pods for this component.<br />When scalingAdapter is enabled, this field is managed by the<br />DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter and should not be modified directly.

  _Minimum: 0_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="multinode" type="MultinodeSpec">
  Multinode is the configuration for multinode components.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="scalingAdapter" type="ScalingAdapter">
  ScalingAdapter configures whether this service uses the DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter.<br />When enabled, replicas are managed via DGDSA and external autoscalers can scale<br />the service using the Scale subresource. When disabled, replicas can be modified directly.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="eppConfig" type="EPPConfig">
  EPPConfig defines EPP-specific configuration options for Endpoint Picker Plugin components.<br />Only applicable when ComponentType is "epp".
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="frontendSidecar" type="FrontendSidecarSpec">
  FrontendSidecar configures an auto-generated frontend sidecar container.<br />When specified, the operator injects a fully configured frontend container<br />with all standard Dynamo environment variables, health probes, and ports.<br />This eliminates the need to manually specify these in extraPodSpec.containers. (GAIE)
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="checkpoint" type="ServiceCheckpointConfig">
  Checkpoint configures container checkpointing for this service.<br />When enabled, pods can be restored from a checkpoint files for faster cold start.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="topologyConstraint" type="TopologyConstraint">
  TopologyConstraint for this service. packDomain is required.<br />When both this and spec.topologyConstraint.packDomain are set, packDomain<br />must be narrower than or equal to the spec-level packDomain.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="gpuMemoryService" type="GPUMemoryServiceSpec">
  GPUMemoryService configures the GPU Memory Service (GMS) sidecar.<br />When enabled, a GMS sidecar is injected and GPU access is managed via DRA.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="failover" type="FailoverSpec">
  Failover configures GMS (GPU Memory Service) failover for this service.<br />For intraPod mode: the main container is cloned into two engine containers (active + standby).<br />For interPod mode: the operator creates a dedicated GMS weight server pod and<br />multiple engine pods per rank that share GPUs via DRA resource claims.
</ParamField>

</ParamField>
<ParamField path="frontendSidecar" type="FrontendSidecarSpec">
  FrontendSidecar configures an auto-generated frontend sidecar container.<br />When specified, the operator injects a fully configured frontend container<br />with all standard Dynamo environment variables, health probes, and ports.<br />This eliminates the need to manually specify these in extraPodSpec.containers. (GAIE)
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="checkpoint" type="ServiceCheckpointConfig">
  Checkpoint configures container checkpointing for this service.<br />When enabled, pods can be restored from a checkpoint files for faster cold start.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="topologyConstraint" type="TopologyConstraint">
  TopologyConstraint for this service. packDomain is required.<br />When both this and spec.topologyConstraint.packDomain are set, packDomain<br />must be narrower than or equal to the spec-level packDomain.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="gpuMemoryService" type="GPUMemoryServiceSpec">
  GPUMemoryService configures the GPU Memory Service (GMS) sidecar.<br />When enabled, a GMS sidecar is injected and GPU access is managed via DRA.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="failover" type="FailoverSpec">
  Failover configures GMS (GPU Memory Service) failover for this service.<br />For intraPod mode: the main container is cloned into two engine containers (active + standby).<br />For interPod mode: the operator creates a dedicated GMS weight server pod and<br />multiple engine pods per rank that share GPUs via DRA resource claims.
</ParamField>

### DynamoGraphDeployment

DynamoGraphDeployment is the Schema for the dynamographdeployments API.

<ParamField path="apiVersion" type="string">
  `nvidia.com/v1alpha1`
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="kind" type="string">
  `DynamoGraphDeployment`
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="metadata" type="ObjectMeta">
  Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of `metadata`.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="spec" type="DynamoGraphDeploymentSpec">
  Spec defines the desired state for this graph deployment.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="status" type="DynamoGraphDeploymentStatus">
  Status reflects the current observed state of this graph deployment.
</ParamField>

### DynamoGraphDeploymentExperimentalSpec

DynamoGraphDeploymentExperimentalSpec groups graph-level opt-in preview
features. Component-level experimental features are represented separately
on component specs.

**Appears in:** [DynamoGraphDeploymentSpec](#dynamographdeploymentspec)

<ParamField path="kvTransferPolicy" type="KvTransferPolicy">
  KvTransferPolicy configures topology-aware routing for KV-cache<br />transfers between prefill and decode workers.
</ParamField>

### DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest

DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest is the Schema for the dynamographdeploymentrequests API.
It serves as the primary interface for users to request model deployments with
specific performance and resource constraints, enabling SLA-driven deployments.

Lifecycle:
 1. Initializing → Pending: Validates spec and prepares for profiling
 2. Pending → Profiling: Creates and runs profiling job (online or AIC)
 3. Profiling → Ready/Deploying: Generates DGD spec after profiling completes
 4. Deploying → Ready: When autoApply=true, monitors DGD until Ready
 5. Ready: Terminal state when DGD is operational or spec is available
 6. DeploymentDeleted: Terminal state when auto-created DGD is manually deleted

The spec becomes immutable once profiling starts. Users must delete and recreate
the DGDR to modify configuration after this point.

DEPRECATION NOTICE: v1alpha1 DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest is deprecated.
Please migrate to nvidia.com/v1beta1 DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest.
v1alpha1 will be removed in a future release.

<ParamField path="apiVersion" type="string">
  `nvidia.com/v1alpha1`
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="kind" type="string">
  `DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest`
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="metadata" type="ObjectMeta">
  Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of `metadata`.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="spec" type="DynamoGraphDeploymentRequestSpec">
  Spec defines the desired state for this deployment request.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="status" type="DynamoGraphDeploymentRequestStatus">
  Status reflects the current observed state of this deployment request.
</ParamField>

### DynamoGraphDeploymentRequestSpec

DynamoGraphDeploymentRequestSpec defines the desired state of a DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest.
This CRD serves as the primary interface for users to request model deployments with
specific performance constraints and resource requirements, enabling SLA-driven deployments.

**Appears in:** [DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest](#dynamographdeploymentrequest)

<ParamField path="model" type="string" required={true}>
  Model specifies the model to deploy (e.g., "Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B", "meta-llama/Llama-3-70b").<br />This is a high-level identifier for easy reference in kubectl output and logs.<br />The controller automatically sets this value in profilingConfig.config.deployment.model.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="backend" type="string" required={true}>
  Backend specifies the inference backend for profiling.<br />The controller automatically sets this value in profilingConfig.config.engine.backend.<br />Profiling runs on real GPUs or via AIC simulation to collect performance data.

  <span className="enum-values"><span className="enum-label">Allowed values:</span> <Badge intent="note" minimal>auto</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>vllm</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>sglang</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>trtllm</Badge></span>
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="useMocker" type="boolean" default="false">
  UseMocker indicates whether to deploy a mocker DynamoGraphDeployment instead of<br />a real backend deployment. When true, the deployment uses simulated engines that<br />don't require GPUs, using the profiling data to simulate realistic timing behavior.<br />Mocker is available in all backend images and useful for large-scale experiments.<br />Profiling still runs against the real backend (specified above) to collect performance data.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="profilingConfig" type="ProfilingConfigSpec" required={true}>
  ProfilingConfig provides the complete configuration for the profiling job.<br />Note: GPU discovery is automatically attempted to detect GPU resources from Kubernetes<br />cluster nodes. If the operator has node read permissions (cluster-wide or explicitly granted),<br />discovered GPU configuration is used as defaults when hardware configuration is not manually<br />specified (minNumGpusPerEngine, maxNumGpusPerEngine, numGpusPerNode). User-specified values<br />always take precedence over auto-discovered values. If GPU discovery fails (e.g.,<br />namespace-restricted operator without node permissions), manual hardware config is required.<br />This configuration is passed directly to the profiler.<br />The structure matches the profile_sla config format exactly (see ProfilingConfigSpec for schema).<br />Note: deployment.model and engine.backend are automatically set from the high-level<br />modelName and backend fields and should not be specified in this config.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="enableGpuDiscovery" type="boolean" default="true">
  EnableGPUDiscovery controls whether the operator attempts to discover GPU hardware from cluster nodes.<br />DEPRECATED: This field is deprecated and will be removed in v1beta1. GPU discovery is now always<br />attempted automatically. Setting this field has no effect - the operator will always try to discover<br />GPU hardware when node read permissions are available. If discovery is unavailable (e.g., namespace-scoped<br />operator without permissions), manual hardware configuration is required regardless of this setting.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="autoApply" type="boolean" default="false">
  AutoApply indicates whether to automatically create a DynamoGraphDeployment<br />after profiling completes. If false, only the spec is generated and stored in status.<br />Users can then manually create a DGD using the generated spec.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="deploymentOverrides" type="DeploymentOverridesSpec">
  DeploymentOverrides allows customizing metadata for the auto-created DGD.<br />Only applicable when AutoApply is true.
</ParamField>

### DynamoGraphDeploymentRequestStatus

DynamoGraphDeploymentRequestStatus represents the observed state of a DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest.
The controller updates this status as the DGDR progresses through its lifecycle.

**Appears in:** [DynamoGraphDeploymentRequest](#dynamographdeploymentrequest)

<ParamField path="state" type="DGDRState" default="Initializing">
  State is a high-level textual status of the deployment request lifecycle.

  <span className="enum-values"><span className="enum-label">Allowed values:</span> <Badge intent="note" minimal>Initializing</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>Pending</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>Profiling</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>Deploying</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>Ready</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>DeploymentDeleted</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>Failed</Badge></span>
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="backend" type="string">
  Backend is extracted from profilingConfig.config.engine.backend for display purposes.<br />This field is populated by the controller and shown in kubectl output.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="observedGeneration" type="integer">
  ObservedGeneration reflects the generation of the most recently observed spec.<br />Used to detect spec changes and enforce immutability after profiling starts.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="conditions" type="[]Condition">
  Conditions contains the latest observed conditions of the deployment request.<br />Standard condition types include: Validation, Profiling, SpecGenerated, DeploymentReady.<br />Conditions are merged by type on patch updates.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="profilingResults" type="string">
  ProfilingResults contains a reference to the ConfigMap holding profiling data.<br />Format: "configmap/\&lt;name\>"
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="generatedDeployment" type="RawExtension">
  GeneratedDeployment contains the full generated DynamoGraphDeployment specification<br />including metadata, based on profiling results. Users can extract this to create<br />a DGD manually, or it's used automatically when autoApply is true.<br />Stored as RawExtension to preserve all fields including metadata.<br />For mocker backends, this contains the mocker DGD spec.

  _EmbeddedResource: \{\}_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="deployment" type="DeploymentStatus">
  Deployment tracks the auto-created DGD when AutoApply is true.<br />Contains name, namespace, state, and creation status of the managed DGD.
</ParamField>

### DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter

DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter provides a scaling interface for individual services
within a DynamoGraphDeployment. It implements the Kubernetes scale
subresource, enabling integration with HPA, KEDA, and custom autoscalers.

The adapter acts as an intermediary between autoscalers and the DGD,
ensuring that only the adapter controller modifies the DGD's service replicas.
This prevents conflicts when multiple autoscaling mechanisms are in play.

<ParamField path="apiVersion" type="string">
  `nvidia.com/v1alpha1`
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="kind" type="string">
  `DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter`
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="metadata" type="ObjectMeta">
  Refer to Kubernetes API documentation for fields of `metadata`.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="spec" type="DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapterSpec" />
<ParamField path="status" type="DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapterStatus" />

### DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapterSpec

DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapterSpec defines the desired state of DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter

**Appears in:** [DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter](#dynamographdeploymentscalingadapter)

<ParamField path="replicas" type="integer" required={true}>
  Replicas is the desired number of replicas for the target service.<br />This field is modified by external autoscalers (HPA/KEDA/Planner) or manually by users.

  _Minimum: 0_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="dgdRef" type="DynamoGraphDeploymentServiceRef" required={true}>
  DGDRef references the DynamoGraphDeployment and the specific service to scale.
</ParamField>

### DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapterStatus

DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapterStatus defines the observed state of DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter

**Appears in:** [DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapter](#dynamographdeploymentscalingadapter)

<ParamField path="replicas" type="integer">
  Replicas is the current number of replicas for the target service.<br />This is synced from the DGD's service replicas and is required for the scale subresource.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="selector" type="string">
  Selector is a label selector string for the pods managed by this adapter.<br />Required for HPA compatibility via the scale subresource.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="lastScaleTime" type="Time">
  LastScaleTime is the last time the adapter scaled the target service.
</ParamField>

### DynamoGraphDeploymentServiceRef

DynamoGraphDeploymentServiceRef identifies a specific service within a DynamoGraphDeployment

**Appears in:** [DynamoGraphDeploymentScalingAdapterSpec](#dynamographdeploymentscalingadapterspec)

<ParamField path="name" type="string" required={true}>
  Name of the DynamoGraphDeployment

  _MinLength: 1_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="serviceName" type="string" required={true}>
  ServiceName is the key name of the service within the DGD's spec.services map to scale

  _MinLength: 1_
</ParamField>

### DynamoGraphDeploymentSpec

DynamoGraphDeploymentSpec defines the desired state of DynamoGraphDeployment.

**Appears in:** [DynamoGraphDeployment](#dynamographdeployment)

<ParamField path="annotations" type="map[string]string">
  Annotations to propagate to all child resources (PCS, DCD, Deployments, and pod templates).<br />Service-level annotations take precedence over these values.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="labels" type="map[string]string">
  Labels to propagate to all child resources (PCS, DCD, Deployments, and pod templates).<br />Service-level labels take precedence over these values.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="priorityClassName" type="string">
  PriorityClassName is the name of the PriorityClass to use for Grove PodCliqueSets.<br />Requires the Grove pathway.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="pvcs" type="[]PVC">
  PVCs defines a list of persistent volume claims that can be referenced by components.<br />Each PVC must have a unique name that can be referenced in component specifications.

  _MaxItems: 100_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="services" type="map[string]DynamoComponentDeploymentSharedSpec">
  Services are the services to deploy as part of this deployment.

  _MaxProperties: 25_
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="envs" type="[]EnvVar">
  Envs are environment variables applied to all services in the deployment unless<br />overridden by service-specific configuration.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="backendFramework" type="string">
  BackendFramework specifies the backend framework (e.g., "sglang", "vllm", "trtllm").

  <span className="enum-values"><span className="enum-label">Allowed values:</span> <Badge intent="note" minimal>sglang</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>vllm</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>trtllm</Badge></span>
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="restart" type="Restart">
  Restart specifies the restart policy for the graph deployment.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="topologyConstraint" type="SpecTopologyConstraint">
  TopologyConstraint is the deployment-level topology constraint.<br />When set, topologyProfile is required and names the ClusterTopology CR to use.<br />packDomain is optional here — it can be omitted when only services carry constraints.<br />Services without their own topologyConstraint inherit from this value.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="experimental" type="DynamoGraphDeploymentExperimentalSpec">
  Experimental groups graph-level preview features whose API shape and<br />behavior may change in breaking ways between releases.
</ParamField>

### DynamoGraphDeploymentStatus

DynamoGraphDeploymentStatus defines the observed state of DynamoGraphDeployment.

**Appears in:** [DynamoGraphDeployment](#dynamographdeployment)

<ParamField path="observedGeneration" type="integer">
  ObservedGeneration is the most recent generation observed by the controller.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="state" type="DGDState" default="initializing">
  State is a high-level textual status of the graph deployment lifecycle.

  <span className="enum-values"><span className="enum-label">Allowed values:</span> <Badge intent="note" minimal>initializing</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>pending</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>successful</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>failed</Badge></span>
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="conditions" type="[]Condition">
  Conditions contains the latest observed conditions of the graph deployment.<br />The slice is merged by type on patch updates.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="services" type="map[string]ServiceReplicaStatus">
  Services contains per-service replica status information.<br />The map key is the service name from spec.services.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="restart" type="RestartStatus">
  Restart contains the status of the restart of the graph deployment.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="checkpoints" type="map[string]ServiceCheckpointStatus">
  Checkpoints contains per-service checkpoint status information.<br />The map key is the service name from spec.services.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="rollingUpdate" type="RollingUpdateStatus">
  RollingUpdate tracks the progress of operator manged rolling updates.<br />Currently only supported for singl-node, non-Grove deployments (DCD/Deployment).
</ParamField>

**Appears in:** [DynamoModel](#dynamomodel)

<ParamField path="modelName" type="string" required={true}>
  ModelName is the full model identifier (e.g., "meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-lora")
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="baseModelName" type="string" required={true}>
  BaseModelName is the base model identifier that matches the service label<br />This is used to discover endpoints via headless services
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="modelType" type="string" default="base">
  ModelType specifies the type of model (e.g., "base", "lora", "adapter")

  <span className="enum-values"><span className="enum-label">Allowed values:</span> <Badge intent="note" minimal>base</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>lora</Badge> <Badge intent="note" minimal>adapter</Badge></span>
</ParamField>

### EPPConfig

EPPConfig contains configuration for EPP (Endpoint Picker Plugin) components.
EPP is responsible for intelligent endpoint selection and KV-aware routing.

**Appears in:** [DynamoComponentDeploymentSharedSpec](#dynamocomponentdeploymentsharedspec) · [DynamoComponentDeploymentSpec](#dynamocomponentdeploymentspec)

<ParamField path="configMapRef" type="ConfigMapKeySelector">
  ConfigMapRef references a user-provided ConfigMap containing EPP configuration.<br />The ConfigMap should contain EndpointPickerConfig YAML.<br />Mutually exclusive with Config.
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="config" type="EndpointPickerConfig">
  Config allows specifying EPP EndpointPickerConfig directly as a structured object.<br />The operator will marshal this to YAML and create a ConfigMap automatically.<br />Mutually exclusive with ConfigMapRef.<br />One of ConfigMapRef or Config must be specified (no default configuration).<br />Uses the upstream type from github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api-inference-extension

  _Type: object_
</ParamField>

### EndpointInfo

EndpointInfo represents a single endpoint (pod) serving the model

**Appears in:** [DynamoModelStatus](#dynamomodelstatus)

<ParamField path="address" type="string">
  Address is the full address of the endpoint (e.g., "http://10.0.1.5:9090")
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="podName" type="string">
  PodName is the name of the pod serving this endpoint
</ParamField>
<ParamField path="ready" type="boolean">
  Ready indicates whether the endpoint is ready to serve traffic<br />For LoRA models: true if the POST /loras request succeeded with a 2xx status code<br />For base models: always false (no probing performed)
</ParamField>
